subtitle: And the Mad Dog
summary: After the triumphant Pouilly-le-Fort experiment, Pasteur is hailed as a hero and turns his laboratory into a vaccine factory, pushing his assistants Roux, Chamberland, and Thuillier to their limits. But disturbing reports soon arrive: the hastily produced anthrax vaccine is killing some sheep instead of saving them. Koch publishes a devastating critique exposing the vaccine's flaws, and Pasteur responds with bluster rather than candor. France elects him to the Académie Française anyway, where the skeptic Renan welcomes him with a gentle warning about pursuing truth with too much passion.

segments.anchor_sentence: But here stood Louis Pasteur, while his twenty-four immune sheep scampered about among the carcasses of the same number of pitiful dead ones, here stood this man, I say, in a gruesomely gorgeous stage-setting of an immortal drama, and all the world was there to see and to record and to be converted to his own faith in his passionate fight against needless death.

visual.scene_description: Pasteur stands triumphant in the field at Pouilly-le-Fort, surrounded by living vaccinated sheep and the carcasses of unvaccinated ones, as crowds of onlookers and journalists press forward in astonishment.
visual.animation_prompt: Simple linocut print style illustration, bold black outlines, flat muted colors in dusty rose and sage green and ochre, rough textured edges, hand-printed look on natural paper, children's book illustration, a neat-bearded man in round spectacles and formal black frock coat stands upright in a French pastoral field, arms raised, surrounded by living woolly sheep, while several sheep carcasses lie on the ground nearby, a crowd of top-hatted men and journalists with notebooks press closer, rolling green hills and a fence in the background

segments.anchor_sentence: The world received this news and waited, confusedly believing that Pasteur was a kind of Messiah who was going to lift from men the burden of all suffering.

visual.scene_description: Inside the cramped Rue d'Ulm laboratory turned vaccine factory, Roux and Chamberland frantically pour broth from huge bubbling kettles into small glass bottles while Pasteur oversees the chaotic operation.
visual.animation_prompt: Simple linocut print style illustration, bold black outlines, flat muted colors in dusty rose and sage green and ochre, rough textured edges, hand-printed look on natural paper, children's book illustration, interior of a cramped 19th-century Paris laboratory with large iron kettles steaming on burners, two young men in rolled shirtsleeves carefully pour cloudy amber liquid through funnels into rows of small glass bottles on a wooden bench, a bearded man in round spectacles and black frock coat watches intently, shelves of flasks and glass tubing line the stone walls

segments.anchor_sentence: And now at night the chittering of the guinea-pigs and the scurrying fights of the buck-rabbits in their cages were drowned by the eerie noise of mad dogs howling—sinister howls that kept Roux and Chamberland and Thuillier from sleep....

visual.scene_description: The laboratory at night, dimly lit, with cages of guinea pigs and rabbits visible and the dark silhouettes of muzzled dogs in larger cages, while exhausted assistants slump over their microscopes, stacks of complaint letters piling on a desk.
visual.animation_prompt: Simple linocut print style illustration, bold black outlines, flat muted colors in dusty rose and sage green and ochre, rough textured edges, hand-printed look on natural paper, children's book illustration, a dim 19th-century laboratory at night lit by a single oil lamp, wire cages with guinea pigs and rabbits line one wall, larger iron-barred cages hold muzzled dogs with bristling fur, two gaunt young men in shirtsleeves slump exhausted at a bench near a brass compound microscope, scattered envelopes and letters pile on a wooden desk nearby

segments.anchor_sentence: What a searcher this Pasteur was, and yet how little of that fine selfless candor of Socrates or Rabelais is to be found in him.

visual.scene_description: At the Geneva conference, Pasteur gives an impassioned speech at a podium while Koch sits in the audience, arms crossed behind round spectacles and thick mustache, looking skeptical and unimpressed.
visual.animation_prompt: Simple linocut print style illustration, bold black outlines, flat muted colors in dusty rose and sage green and ochre, rough textured edges, hand-printed look on natural paper, children's book illustration, a grand 19th-century European lecture hall with arched windows, a gray-haired bearded man in round spectacles and black frock coat gestures dramatically at a wooden podium, in the front row a stocky man with thick dark mustache and round wire spectacles sits with arms folded looking skeptical, rows of formally dressed scientists fill tiered wooden seats behind them

segments.anchor_sentence: Then Pasteur went through the roof and answered Koch's cool facts in an amazing paper with arguments that would not have fooled the jury of a country debating society.

visual.scene_description: Pasteur's admission ceremony at the Académie Française, with the large, serene Renan welcoming the gray-haired, paralyzed Pasteur from a grand chair, the ornate hall filled with formally dressed Immortals.
visual.animation_prompt: Simple linocut print style illustration, bold black outlines, flat muted colors in dusty rose and sage green and ochre, rough textured edges, hand-printed look on natural paper, children's book illustration, an ornate domed hall of the Académie Française with gilded columns and velvet curtains, a very large heavyset man in formal dark robes sits in an elaborate carved chair gesturing calmly, facing a thin gray-haired man with round spectacles in black frock coat who leans slightly on a cane, rows of men in dark formal dress observe from curved wooden benches
